raw_input:
[5.0/5] Smells Great and Works Great on your Face! This product is the first cleansing oil I've used on my face. I'm really enjoying it. It easily removes makeup and leaves me with a soft face and no break-outs after removing my make-up. My nineteen year old son, JUST came up to me and gave me a hug good-bye and commented on how soft my face was. True story! This hasn't happened before, and the only difference to my cleansing regimen has been this cleansing oil. So far I'm loving it. It easily and smoothly goes on my face and removes my make-up. The cleansing oil smells nice, it's using light, nice smelling oils and feels good on my face. I highly recommend it. [3.0/5] Nice I have been using a cleansing oil as step one in a double cleanse routine for 20 years now. Back then there was only one company from Japan that made such a thing that was available in the US. So for years, that is what I used. I decided to try this product simply to try something new. It has the same consistency as my normal cleansing oil, but didn't seem to lather or clean as well. It also left my skin feeling oily despite how much a rinsed it away. It has a fresh, citrus scent and the bottle size it a tad bit bigger than my normal. That being said, I liked this product but probably would not purchase to use again. The cost is higher and I can't get past the fact it doesn't clean off makeup that well. [5.0/5] A little goes a long way. This cleansing oil has an almost citrus smell so I thought it might be too much for my sensitive skin. I cannot use most products. I have not had a problem with this one One squirt is more than enough to remove even waterproof mascara along with cleansing your entire face. I am 64 and this leaves my skin silky smooth and clean. I massage the oil into my skin, add a bit of water and continue to massage it into my skin until becomes white and begins to break down. You will feel it change as you go, then rinse it off with warm water. I am very happy to find this product. It seems that cleansing oils are fading away after being the rage for awhile. I wish I had known about cleansing oil all my adult life. I will continue using A24 Cleansing Oil cleansing oil. [5.0/5] Doesn't leave my skin feeling oily I have another brand like this and love it. I've used this one a couple of times and it's just like my other one. I use a jade gua sha stone with it. Doesn't leave my skin feeling oily. Nice sunflower scent. Lathers up with warm water. Will definitely keep using it.
I like that the pump can be closed so there are no accidental spills. [5.0/5] This Makeup Remover Really Works! This A24 Cleansing Oil really works! I usually use baby oil to remove makeup, but this A24 Cleansing Oil
was even more effective! Like the baby oil, it is hypoallergenic. However, this cleansing oil does not contain mineral oil, a petroleum product. Instead, it contains USDA-approved organic Aloe Vera leaf juice. Camellia Japonica Seed Oil, Shea, Safflower oil, jojoba oil, avocado oil, Argan oil, Macadamia seed oil, olive oil, marigold, green tea, chamomile, horsetail, acai, lavender, ginseng, rosemary, sage, comfrey, nettle, quinoa, orange peel oil and grapefruit peel oil. All good things! And no water is added.

This wonderfully effective oil is also great because of the ingredients it does not contain! There are no parabens, silicones, phthalates, alcohol or artificial colors or fragrances.

This awesome makeup remover is made in the United States. [5.0/5] Great cleanser This is a gentle cleanser for all face types. I have oily skin and I can use this product without it making my face oiler. It feels like it really cleans my skin as though it’s really getting into the pores. I don’t have to wash my face twice to remove all dirt from it. [5.0/5] Softens skin, it's not greasy. I am using this because of the deep cleaning claim and not to remove makeup. I live in the mountains where it is windy and dry, and I have mild eczema so I am always battling dry skin. I really had my doubts that an oil based product could clean my skin... well, I just discovered that yes it can! I am probably not using it as directed, as I like to wet wash my face while I am in the shower. Standing over my bathroom sink just makes too much of a mess. When it first lathers up it sort of has a smell like citronella, which I am not fond of, but I found that smell to quickly dissipate, and end up smelling quite nice and fresh, and not greasy. My skin feels soft and clean after using this product. It it was a larger bottle i might use it up on the rest of my dry skin body, if it wasn't so costly! (Sorry, TMI!) [5.0/5] Mild yet powerful cleansing oil This is what I use as the first step to my night time skin care routine. It is a gentle yet powerful cleansing oil that effectively removes layers of sunscreen, waterproof mascara, liquid eyeliner, liquid glitter eyeshadow, and eyebrow pomade at once. I haven’t tried this with liquid foundation yet, so I cannot say much about it’s ability to remove a full-face make-up.

What I love about it is the texture. It is not a watery nor a heavy type of cleansing oil. It is rather lightweight that easily glides onto the skin for a gentle massage. Two pumps of this cleansing oil is enough to cleanse my entire face thoroughly without leaving any traces of waterproof make-up and sunscreen. It also doesn’t leave any greasy or sticky residue.

On its own, it is a hydrating and refreshing cleanser. I have an oily skin, so I love how I feel very refreshed after using this; it helps remove excess oil and unclog my pores. I noticed that my nose where I have the most blackheads is clearer than before.

Rather than using cleansing wipes, I have always used cleansing oils and balms because these truly remove make-up and sunscreen. I also love that cleansing oils and balms are environmentally-friendly without leaving any wastes. However, not all cleansing oils and balms are created equal. I feel that this A24 cleansing oil is very luxurious due to its premium ingredients. It is the only cleansing oil that I have tried that can be used on its own without a second or follow-up foam/gel cleanser.

I strongly recommend this product to anyone in need of a cleansing oil for hydration and thorough removal of make-up and sunscreen. [5.0/5] Highly effective My wife says this makeup remover is one of the most effective she's used. Her initial impression was that it might be something that she wouldn't want to continue using because it's extremely oily. She was worried it would leave an oily residue on her skin after using it. However, despite going on very oily, it leaves no nasty residue and washes away quickly and easily, taking makeup off with it. She says her skin feels pleasant and cleansed after using this, and it's far less harsh than many chemical makeup removers. [3.0/5] Super oily This makeup remover is really oily. Leaves my face feeling really oily. Also, it does not remove waterproof eye makeup well at all. [3.0/5] Effective but Messy I currently use another oil-based cleanser for my face so I was excited to try this one. It sounds funny to say an oil cleans without leaving your skin greasy but it does just that. In comparison to the other, this one cleans just as well, however, I don't like that I have to lather the oil to make it work. It causes too much of a mess when cleaning my face at the sink. I will have to use this cleanser exclusively in the shower. I will say, there's a bit of confusion in the instructions. It says to clean makeup around eyes but then it says to keep away from eyes? Other reviewers said they used it to remove eye makeup so I gave it a try. I did not experience any stinging or irritation. [5.0/5] Very mild and worked out great for my oily sensitive skin. I requested this cleansing oil due to its claim on natural ingredients (99.75%).
First of all, I have an oily combination skin type that gets sensitive depending on my hormonal cycle. So naturally I have been hesitant to use any oil type skin care products on my face.
But I have learned that using an oily skin cleanser will not produce more oil on your skin, instead, a good oil cleanser is designed to balance and remove everything but skin's moisture.
So I have been searching for one to try out. Since this one is my first cleansing oil, I don’t have any experience with other cleansing oils to compare this with. So my impression about this is as a general cleansing product whether oil or otherwise.
The bottom line is that I liked how it worked on removing make-ups and how my skin felt afterwards. It didn’t feel heavy on my oily skin and felt soft and clean after washing my face following this. [5.0/5] Great two-in-one makeup remover & cleanser I love this cleanser. It’s a great one-stop-shop to remove makeup and wash my face. The oil removes liquid eyeliner and mascara, along with foundation. It leaves my skin feeling clean and fresh—and the best part is I don’t need a second cleanser to remove that oily makeup remover feeling I get with other products. The smell is mild, and though I have sensitive skin, this oil to foam cleanser is safe and gentle. [5.0/5] Works great! I bought this to replace a different oil cleanser that went out of stock and I’m really happy with the product. It removes eye makeup, including mascara, and my foundation. It has a refreshing citrus scent that’s mild and not overwhelming. I like that it only uses clean, natural ingredients. I have sensitive skin and this felt nice on my skin—and didn’t cause a reaction like so many products out there do.

response:
Here is a summary of the product reviews:

The A24 Cleansing Oil is generally very well received, with most reviewers giving it 5 out of 5 stars. The majority of users praise its ability to effectively remove makeup, including waterproof mascara and eyeliner, while leaving skin feeling soft, clean, and non-greasy. Many note that a small amount goes a long way, and that it works well for sensitive and oily skin types. The mild citrus scent is frequently mentioned as a positive. Several reviewers appreciate the natural, clean ingredient list, which includes oils like jojoba, argan, and avocado, along with organic aloe vera, and no parabens, silicones, or artificial fragrances. Users also like that it functions as a two-in-one makeup remover and cleanser, often eliminating the need for a second cleansing step.

On the negative side, a small number of reviewers felt the product left their skin feeling too oily and did not rinse off as cleanly as competing products. One reviewer found the lathering process messy when used at a sink, preferring to use it in the shower. A couple of users also felt it did not remove waterproof makeup as effectively as expected. The higher price point was noted as a drawback by at least one reviewer.

Overall, the product is highly recommended by most users, particularly for those with sensitive or oily skin.
